 {"id":"bd-3y1n","title":"EPIC: No-Brainer ACFS Improvements (Jan 2026)","description":"## Epic: No-Brainer ACFS Improvements (Jan 2026)

This epic contains 15 high-impact, low-complexity improvements to the ACFS project that will obviously benefit users with minimal risk. Each improvement was evaluated against these criteria:

**Evaluation Criteria:**
- Robust: Does it make the system more resilient?
- Reliable: Does it reduce failure modes?
- Performant: Does it make things faster?
- Intuitive: Is it discoverable and obvious?
- User-friendly: Does it reduce friction?
- Ergonomic: Does it fit natural workflows?
- Useful: Does it solve real problems?
- Compelling: Will users be glad it exists?
- Accretive: Does it build on existing strengths?
- Pragmatic: Is the effort justified by the benefit?

## Top 5 (Highest Impact)
1. **bd-3nbx**: `acfs doctor --fix` - Auto-remediation mode
2. **bd-2dkb**: Copy-to-clipboard for web wizard code blocks
3. **bd-29fl**: `acfs status` - One-line health summary
4. **bd-zhdi**: Shell tab completion for acfs command
5. **bd-2gys**: Step validation in web wizard

## Next 10 (High Value, Complementary)
6. **bd-21kh**: Progress bar during tool installation
7. **bd-39ye**: NO_COLOR environment variable support
8. **bd-1lug**: Rate limit backoff for GitHub API calls
9. **bd-1eop**: Skip already-installed tools during install
10. **bd-w8fx**: VPS provider comparison table
11. **bd-1yfv**: "I'm stuck" help button in web wizard
12. **bd-2p56**: `acfs changelog` command
13. **bd-331g**: Dark mode toggle in web wizard
14. **bd-2zqr**: Webhook notification on install completion
15. **bd-3jd9**: `acfs export-config` command

## Implementation Notes
- Each bead is self-contained with full implementation details
- Start with top 5 for maximum early impact
- Beads 6-10 are quick wins that complement the top 5
- Beads 11-15 are nice-to-haves that round out the experience

## Success Criteria
- All 15 beads implemented and tested
- No regressions in existing functionality
- Documentation updated for new features
- User feedback collected on improvements","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:00:18.624398983Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T22:33:42.559881815Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T22:33:39.119946333Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":37,"issue_id":"bd-3y1n","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Closing EPIC to unblock 13 child tasks. bd-2dkb (copy-to-clipboard) completed.  {"id":"bd-47sq","title":"Deep exploration: MS (Meta Skill)","description":"## Goal
Perform deep exploration of MS (Meta Skill) and revise its description on the flywheel/TLDR pages with comprehensive testing.

## Phase 0: Pre-flight Verification (CRITICAL)

### 0.1 Tool Existence Check
```bash
# Verify MS installation
[[ -d /dp/meta_skill ]] && echo "PASS: ms repo exists" || { echo "FAIL: ms repo missing"; exit 1; }
command -v ms &>/dev/null && echo "PASS: ms command available" || { echo "FAIL: ms not in PATH"; exit 1; }

# Check Claude Code skill integration
[[ -d ~/.claude/skills ]] && echo "INFO: Claude skills directory exists" || echo "INFO: No skills directory yet"
```

### 0.2 Content Snapshot (BEFORE State)
```bash
SNAPSHOT_DIR=/tmp/ms-exploration-snapshots-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
mkdir -p $SNAPSHOT_DIR
cp apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before
cp apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before
echo "Snapshots saved to $SNAPSHOT_DIR"
```

### 0.3 TypeScript Interface Reference
Content must match FlywheelTool interface:
- id, name, tagline, description, deepDescription
- features[], cliCommands[], connectsTo[]
- integrationLevel, category, status

## Phase 1: Research (GATHER CONTEXT)

### 1.1 Primary Documentation
- `cat /dp/meta_skill/README.md` - Read full README
- Check for skill authoring docs, SKILL.md format

### 1.2 Code Investigation
- Launch code investigation agent to understand:
  - Skill creation workflow
  - SKILL.md format and requirements
  - Skill validation mechanism
  - Installation into Claude Code
  - Skill discovery and loading

### 1.3 CLI Command Verification
```bash
# Verify each documented command works
ms --help 2>&1 | head -20
ms create --help 2>&1 | head -10
ms validate --help 2>&1 | head -10

# Test actual functionality
ms list 2>&1 | head -10
```

### 1.4 External Context Search
- `/xf search 'meta skill OR ms OR skill authoring'` - Twitter archive
- `cass search 'ms skill create' --robot --limit 10` - Past sessions

### 1.5 Project State Review
- Check beads in /dp/meta_skill/.beads/
- Review recent commits: `cd /dp/meta_skill && git log --oneline -20`

## Phase 2: Analysis (SYNTHESIZE UNDERSTANDING)

### 2.1 Core Capabilities Verification
Document findings for each area:
- [ ] Skill creation workflow
- [ ] SKILL.md format specification
- [ ] Validation rules and errors
- [ ] Installation process
- [ ] Skill discovery mechanism

### 2.2 Synergy Verification
Cross-reference these tools actually integrate:
- [ ] Claude Code - primary integration target
- [ ] sc (skill) - skill creation helper
- [ ] sw (skill writer) - advanced skill authoring

### 2.3 Format Verification
```bash
# Check SKILL.md template
cat /dp/meta_skill/templates/SKILL.md 2>/dev/null | head -30
# Or find example skills
find /dp -name "SKILL.md" 2>/dev/null | head -5 | xargs head -20
```

## Phase 3: Revision (UPDATE DESCRIPTIONS)

### 3.1 Update apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
Update ms entry with VERIFIED information:
- `tagline`: Skill authoring for Claude Code
- `description`: Creation and validation
- `deepDescription`: How skill system works
- `features`: Verified capabilities
- `cliCommands`: Only commands that actually work
- `connectsTo`: Only verified integrations

### 3.2 Update apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
Update TldrFlywheelTool entry with:
- `briefDescription`: Technical summary
- `bulletPoints`: Verified capabilities
- `synergyExamples`: Working integration examples

## Phase 4: Testing (VERIFY CHANGES)

### 4.1 TypeScript Compilation
```bash
cd apps/web && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20
```

### 4.2 Unit Tests
```bash
# Test ms entry structure
node -e "
const { flywheelTools } = require('./lib/flywheel');
const ms = flywheelTools.find(t => t.id === 'ms');
console.log('Testing ms entry...');
console.assert(ms, 'ms entry exists');
console.assert(ms.features?.length > 0, 'has features');
console.assert(ms.cliCommands?.length > 0, 'has commands');
console.log('All assertions passed');
"
```

### 4.3 E2E Test: Skill Operations
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG=/tmp/ms-e2e-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log

echo "=== MS E2E Test ===" | tee $LOG
echo "Started: $(date)" | tee -a $LOG

# Test 1: List skills
echo "Test 1: List skills..." | tee -a $LOG
ms list 2>&1 | head -10 | tee -a $LOG

# Test 2: Validate a skill
echo "Test 2: Validate..." | tee -a $LOG
ms validate /dp/meta_skill 2>&1 | head -10 | tee -a $LOG || echo "Validation done"

# Test 3: Create help
echo "Test 3: Create help..." | tee -a $LOG
ms create --help 2>&1 | head -5 | tee -a $LOG

echo "=== All Tests Passed ===" | tee -a $LOG
echo "Log: $LOG"
```

### 4.4 Content Diff Verification
```bash
echo "=== Changes Made ==="
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts || true
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts || true
```

## Phase 5: Completion (FINALIZE)

### 5.1 Rollback Procedure (if tests fail)
```bash
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
echo "Rolled back to pre-exploration state"
```

### 5.2 Sync Changes
```bash
br update bd-47sq --status done
br sync --flush-only
```

### 5.3 Final Verification
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors
- [ ] Skill format documented correctly
- [ ] No broken links or references","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:01:36.485651577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T03:32:05.943130894Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T03:32:05.943105255Z","close_reason":"MS deep exploration complete: verified v0.1.0, 60+ commands, 12 MCP tools (corrected from 6).  {"id":"bd-eh383","title":"meta_skill ARM64 Linux binary missing — blocked upstream (GH #115)","description":"## Context
GitHub issue #115 filed by tayiorbeii (2026-02-06). On ARM64 Linux (aarch64), meta_skill install 404s because v0.1.0 has no aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu binary.

## Available binaries in v0.1.0:
- ms-0.1.0-aarch64-apple-darwin.tar.gz (ARM64 macOS - exists)
- ms-0.1.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz (x86_64 Linux - exists)
- ms-0.1.0-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.zip (Windows - exists)
- aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu — MISSING

## Two-Part Fix

### Part 1: ACFS workaround (THIS repo, can do now)
Update doctor.sh check at lines 1115-1116 to detect aarch64 on Linux and show:
  "meta_skill: ARM64 Linux binary not yet available (see https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/meta_skill/issues/1)"
instead of the generic install suggestion that will 404.

Implementation:
```bash
# In doctor.sh, before the meta_skill install suggestion:
if [[ "$(uname -m)" == "aarch64" ]] && [[ "$(uname -s)" == "Linux" ]]; then
    log_item "warn" "meta_skill (ms)" "ARM64 Linux binary not yet available"
    return 0  # Skip install attempt
fi
```

### Part 2: Upstream (meta_skill repo, blocked)
Add aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu to the GoReleaser or release CI matrix in Dicklesworthstone/meta_skill. Requires:
- Cross-compilation target or dedicated ARM64 runner
- New release (v0.1.1 or v0.2.0)
This is blocked on upstream release work.

## Testing
After Part 1:
1. On this VPS (x86_64): `acfs doctor` should still show meta_skill normally
2. Simulate ARM64: mock uname output or grep the code to verify the aarch64 check exists
3. Verify the issue URL is correct and accessible

## Key Files
- scripts/lib/doctor.sh (lines 1109-1117, meta_skill check)
- Upstream: Dicklesworthstone/meta_skill release CI matrix

## Status
Part 1 is actionable now. Part 2 is blocked upstream. Keep this bead open until both parts are done.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:16:39.015874050Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:39:31.599422934Z","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup#115","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["arm64","bug","meta_skill","upstream-blocked"]}

GitHub issue #125 filed by joyshmitz (2026-02-08). The install path was fixed (commit 4d0174f0 — agents.sh now uses @latest), but update.sh still calls bare "claude update" which forces the stable channel.

## Problem
run_cmd_claude_update() in scripts/lib/update.sh (line 1230) calls bare "claude update" at lines 1254, 1260, 1267, 1269, 1274. This always uses the stable channel. On a SUCCESSFUL update, users get silently downgraded from latest (e.g., 2.1.37) to stable (e.g., 2.1.25). The fallback path (update_run_verified_installer claude latest at line 1157) correctly uses latest, but only fires when "claude update" FAILS.

Additionally, the uca alias in acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc line 158 uses bare "~/.local/bin/claude update" (also stable channel). The full alias chains claude update with codex and gemini updates: alias uca='~/.local/bin/claude update && (bun install -g --trust @openai/codex@latest || bun install -g --trust @openai/codex) && bun install -g --trust @google/gemini-cli@latest'

## CRITICAL: --channel flag DOES NOT EXIST
Verified by running: claude update --help => only -h/--help available. The originally proposed fix (claude update --channel latest) is INVALID. This was confirmed by running the actual command on this VPS.

## Chosen Fix: Replace run_cmd_claude_update() with verified installer
The update_run_verified_installer function already exists at update.sh:715-737 and correctly handles the latest channel. It:
1. Looks up the installer URL from KNOWN_INSTALLERS[claude] = "https://claude.ai/install.sh" (security.sh:183)
2. Fetches the installer script
3. Verifies its SHA-256 checksum against checksums.yaml
4. Pipes to bash with args (e.g., "latest")
The fallback path at line 1157 already uses this: run_cmd "Claude Code (reinstall)" update_run_verified_installer claude latest

The fix replaces the entire run_cmd_claude_update() function body with a call to update_run_verified_installer, preserving the logging/error-handling wrapper while switching the underlying mechanism from bare "claude update" to the verified installer with the "latest" argument.

For the uca alias, replace the "~/.local/bin/claude update" portion with an inline verified-installer invocation, OR redirect through a wrapper script, OR call the update function. The alias must preserve the codex and gemini update chain.

## Affected Files

+{"id":"bd-gsjqf.2","title":"Rewrite run_cmd_claude_update() to use verified installer","description":"## What
Replace the body of `run_cmd_claude_update()` (update.sh lines 1230-1296) so it calls `update_run_verified_installer claude latest` instead of bare `claude update`. This is the core fix for GH #125.

## Why
`claude update` (bare) always uses the stable channel. There is NO `--channel` flag (verified: `claude update --help` shows only `-h/--help`). The function currently calls bare `claude update` at 5 separate locations (lines 1254, 1260, 1267, 1269, 1274), all of which silently downgrade users from the latest channel (e.g., 2.1.37) to stable (e.g., 2.1.25).

The `update_run_verified_installer` function (update.sh lines 715-737) already exists and correctly handles the latest channel by:
1. Looking up installer URL from `KNOWN_INSTALLERS[claude]` = `https://claude.ai/install.sh` (security.sh:183)
2. Fetching the installer script
3. Verifying its SHA-256 checksum against checksums.yaml
4. Piping to `bash -s -- latest` (the "latest" arg selects the channel)

This function is already used as the fallback at line 1157: `run_cmd "Claude Code (reinstall)" update_run_verified_installer claude latest`

## Current Code (lines 1230-1296)
```bash
run_cmd_claude_update() {
    local desc="Claude Code (native update)"
    local cmd_display="claude update"
    log_to_file "Running: $cmd_display"
    if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == "true" ]]; then
        log_item "skip" "$desc" "dry-run: $cmd_display"
        return 0
    fi
    log_item "run" "$desc"
    local exit_code=0
    # ... 5 branches each calling bare "claude update" ...
    # Lines 1254, 1260, 1267, 1269, 1274
}
```

## Proposed Fix
Replace the 5-branch if/elif structure with a single call to `update_run_verified_installer`. Preserve the logging/status/dry-run wrapper:

```bash
run_cmd_claude_update() {
    local desc="Claude Code (verified installer update)"
    local cmd_display="update_run_verified_installer claude latest"
    log_to_file "Running: $cmd_display"
    if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == "true" ]]; then
        log_item "skip" "$desc" "dry-run: $cmd_display"
        return 0
    fi
    log_item "run" "$desc"
    local exit_code=0
    local output=""
    output=$(update_run_verified_installer claude latest 2>&1) || exit_code=$?
    [[ -n "$output" ]] && log_to_file "Output: $output"
    if [[ "$VERBOSE" == "true" ]] && [[ "$QUIET" \!= "true" ]]; then
        [[ -n "$output" ]] && echo "$output"
    fi
    if [[ -n "${UPDATE_LOG_FILE:-}" ]] && [[ -n "$output" ]]; then
```

+{"id":"bd-gsjqf.3","title":"Fix uca alias and any other bare "claude update" occurrences found in audit","description":"## What
Fix the `uca` alias in `acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc` line 158 and any other bare `claude update` occurrences discovered by the audit (bd-gsjqf.1). Each occurrence needs a fix appropriate to its context.

## Why
The uca alias is a convenience shortcut that chains 3 update commands. It currently starts with bare `~/.local/bin/claude update` which uses the stable channel. This is a separate occurrence from the `run_cmd_claude_update()` function — fixing update.sh alone is insufficient because users who type `uca` directly still get downgraded.

## Current uca Alias (acfs.zshrc:158)
```bash
alias uca='~/.local/bin/claude update && (bun install -g --trust @openai/codex@latest || bun install -g --trust @openai/codex) && bun install -g --trust @google/gemini-cli@latest'
```

## CRITICAL: Preserve the Full Chain
The alias updates THREE tools, not just Claude:
1. `~/.local/bin/claude update` — Claude Code (BROKEN: stable channel)
2. `bun install -g --trust @openai/codex@latest || bun install -g --trust @openai/codex` — Codex CLI
3. `bun install -g --trust @google/gemini-cli@latest` — Gemini CLI

A naive fix that replaces only the Claude part and drops the rest would DESTROY codex and gemini updates. This was caught in review round 4 and must not regress.

## Fix Options for uca Alias

### Option A: Inline the verified installer (simplest)
```bash
alias uca='(curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash -s -- latest) && (bun install -g --trust @openai/codex@latest || bun install -g --trust @openai/codex) && bun install -g --trust @google/gemini-cli@latest'
```
**Pro**: Self-contained, no function dependency. **Con**: No SHA-256 verification (security.sh checksum not available in alias context). Hardcodes URL.

### Option B: Call acfs-update with --only flag (if available)
```bash
alias uca='acfs-update --only=claude,codex,gemini'
```
**Pro**: Single source of truth. **Con**: Need to verify `--only` flag exists. Changes alias semantics.

### Option C: Source update functions and call directly
```bash
alias uca='source ~/.config/acfs/scripts/lib/update.sh && run_cmd_claude_update && (bun install -g --trust @openai/codex@latest || ...) && ...'
```
**Pro**: Uses the fixed function. **Con**: Sourcing entire update.sh is heavyweight for an alias.

### Option D: Create a thin wrapper script
Create `~/.local/bin/update-claude-latest` that wraps `update_run_verified_installer claude latest`, then:
```bash
alias uca='update-claude-latest && (bun install -g --trust @openai/codex@latest || bun install -g --trust @openai/codex) && bun install -g --trust @google/gemini-cli@latest'
```
**Pro**: Clean separation, verified installer. **Con**: Another script to maintain.

### Recommendation
Option A is the pragmatic choice for now — it matches what the verified installer does (same URL) minus the SHA-256 check, which is acceptable for interactive user-initiated updates. The security concern is lower for aliases (user is actively running it) vs automated update.sh (runs unattended). Add a comment above the alias noting this.

+{"id":"bd-gsjqf.5","title":"End-to-end verification and close GH #125","description":"## What
Final end-to-end verification that the complete fix works on the live system, then close GitHub issue #125.

## Why
This is the gate task that verifies everything works together before we close the issue. It runs AFTER all code changes (bd-gsjqf.2, bd-gsjqf.3) and AFTER testing (bd-gsjqf.4).

## CRITICAL: How to Verify Channel
`claude --version` outputs `2.1.37 (Claude Code)` — it does NOT show the channel name. To verify:
```bash
# Get dist-tags (shows version per channel)
npm view @anthropic-ai/claude-code dist-tags
# => { stable: '2.1.25', latest: '2.1.37', next: '2.1.37' }

# Get installed version
claude --version | awk '{print $1}'
# => 2.1.37

# Compare: if installed matches latest dist-tag, we are on latest channel
```

## E2E Verification Steps

### Step 1: Record baseline
```bash
echo "=== Baseline ==="
claude --version
npm view @anthropic-ai/claude-code dist-tags
echo "Current version should match 'latest' dist-tag, NOT 'stable'"
```

### Step 2: Run the test suite first
```bash
bash scripts/tests/test_update_channel.sh
# Must exit 0 with all tests passing
```

### Step 3: Run full ACFS update (live)
```bash
# Run with verbose to see which update mechanism is used
acfs-update --verbose 2>&1 | tee /tmp/acfs_update_e2e.log
```

### Step 4: Verify in update log
```bash
# Check logs for evidence of verified installer
grep -i "verified\|installer\|install.sh" /tmp/acfs_update_e2e.log
# Should show verified installer was used
grep -i "claude update" /tmp/acfs_update_e2e.log
# Should NOT show bare "claude update" as an executed command
# (may appear in log messages/variable values, that is OK)
```

### Step 5: Verify version after update
```bash
echo "=== Post-update ==="
claude --version
npm view @anthropic-ai/claude-code dist-tags
installed=$(claude --version | awk '{print $1}')
latest=$(npm view @anthropic-ai/claude-code dist-tags 2>/dev/null | grep -oP "latest: '\K[^']+")
if [[ "$installed" == "$latest" ]]; then
    echo "PASS: version $installed matches latest channel"
else
    echo "FAIL: version $installed does NOT match latest ($latest)"
fi
```

### Step 6: Test uca alias (live)
```bash
# Source the alias
source ~/.config/acfs/acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc 2>/dev/null
# Verify alias definition
type uca
# Run it (this will actually update - be prepared)
uca
# Verify still on latest
claude --version
```

### Step 7: Idempotency check
```bash
# Run update again — should not downgrade
acfs-update --verbose 2>&1 | tee /tmp/acfs_update_e2e_2.log
installed_after=$(claude --version | awk '{print $1}')
echo "Version after second update: $installed_after (should still be latest)"
```

## Close GH #125
Once all verification passes:
```bash

See bd-gsjqf epic and sub-tasks (.1-.5) for full implementation details."
```
